title: Tomato Sauce on Linen
timeEstimate: 20–60 minutes; oil-strip + tannin acid + peroxide on white linen, sunlight finisher
stainChemistry: Tomato sauce on linen is a three-part combo: tomato red pigment (lycopene, fat-soluble), plant tannin, and oil (olive oil + cooking oils). The oil is the key: lycopene is fat-soluble, which means it's locked behind the oil until the oil is stripped. Order matters: Dawn FIRST (oil-strip), then vinegar (tannin acid), then 3% peroxide on white linen for residual color, then sunlight finisher (UV photodegrades lycopene). Skip the oil-strip and peroxide can't reach the pigment — that's why tomato stains plateau at faint orange. Two hard rules: NO hot water (sets tannin and lycopene) and NO alkaline cleaners (baking soda, ammonia, OxiClean alone before peroxide DARKEN tannin permanently). Structured / lined / dry-clean-only / vintage linen = pro.
whyThisWorks: Dawn strips the oil carrier holding lycopene in place. Once the oil is gone, vinegar disrupts the tannin grip. 3% peroxide on white linen partially oxidizes lycopene. Sunlight is the most reliable finisher — UV photodegrades lycopene over hours. Cool wash plus AIR-DRY FLAT (linen wrinkles aggressively wet) finishes. The honest cap on dyed linen: faint orange shadow may remain.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): tomato sauce is lycopene (fat-soluble carotenoid) + tannin polyphenols + olive/canola oil. Pro uses NSD oil-strip + acid-side tannin spotter + 3% H₂O₂ + UV-assist + acetic acid neutralizing rinse.
defaultAssumption: Pasta night / pizza / Italian dinner spill on a simple flat washable linen napkin or shirt. Fresh = full sequence has a real shot. Hot-dried = often a faint orange shadow. Structured / lined / vintage linen = pro from minute one.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen = pro from minute one. Simple flat washable linen = home candidate. Scrape solid sauce off with plastic spoon edge — never rub.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold running water, white catch towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Hold the BACK of the spot under cold running water. 60–90 sec. Cold only — heat sets tannin and lycopene.

spottingProtocol.technique: Strip oil so peroxide can reach pigment
spottingProtocol.instruction: Generous Dawn. Soft toothbrush 5–10 min. CRITICAL — without oil-strip, peroxide can't reach lycopene and the orange stays. Damp-cloth rinse.

spottingProtocol.equipment: 1:1 white vinegar + cool water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Vinegar + cool water on damp cloth, press 5–10 min. Damp-cloth rinse THOROUGHLY before any peroxide.
WHITE linen only. Hidden test on dyed. 3% peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min. NEVER mix with chlorine bleach, ammonia, or alkaline cleaners.
Hang in direct sunlight while damp. UV breaks down residual lycopene — most reliable finisher for tomato. Multi-day for stubborn cases.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Washing machine, cool water, drying rack
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cool machine wash. AIR-DRY FLAT — linen wrinkles aggressively when hung wet. NEVER hot dryer until cleared.

steps: 1. Care label triage + scrape solids gently.
3. Dawn oil-strip cool 5–10 min — MANDATORY before peroxide.
4. White vinegar 1:1 cool 5–10 min — rinse thoroughly before peroxide.
5. 3% H₂O₂ for white linen, hidden test on dyed.
6. Sunlight finisher — UV photodegrades residual orange.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat sets tannin and lycopene
warnings: Structured / lined / vintage linen = pro from minute one
Oil-strip BEFORE peroxide — Dawn first or orange persists
NEVER hot water — sets tannin and lycopene
NEVER alkaline cleaners — DARKEN tannin permanently
Linen air-dries FLAT — hanging stretches the fiber
Hidden test on dyed linen before peroxide

homeSolutions.instruction: Tomato sauce stains because the red pigment (lycopene) is fat-soluble — water alone can't touch it. The oil in the sauce holds the pigment in the fiber. Order matters: Dawn FIRST to strip the oil, then vinegar for the tannin, then 3% peroxide on white linen for residual color, then sunlight finisher. Skip the Dawn step and the orange ghost stays. Two hard rules: NO hot water (sets tannin and lycopene) and NO alkaline cleaners (baking soda, ammonia, OxiClean alone darken tannin permanently).

Dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen = pro from minute one. Simple flat washable linen (napkin, plain shirt, basic tablecloth) = home candidate. Scrape solid sauce off with a plastic spoon edge — flat-edge work only, never rub.
Turn the linen inside out. Hold the BACK of the spot under cold running water with a white towel underneath. 60 to 90 seconds. Cold only — heat sets tannin and lycopene into the fiber.

Generous Dawn dish soap directly on the spot. Work in with a soft toothbrush for 5 to 10 minutes. This step is MANDATORY — without it, the oil coats the lycopene and peroxide can't reach the pigment. Damp-cloth rinse.

Mix 1 part white vinegar with 1 part cool water. Apply with a damp cloth, press 5 to 10 minutes. Disrupts the tannin grip. Damp-cloth rinse THOROUGHLY before any peroxide — acid plus peroxide degrades the peroxide.
WHITE linen only: 3% peroxide on a damp cloth, press 5 to 10 minutes, blot. Hidden-area test on dyed linen first.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach, ammonia, or alkaline cleaners — toxic fumes.

homeSolutions.instruction: Hang the damp linen in direct sunlight for several hours. UV breaks down residual lycopene. This is the most reliable finisher for tomato — patience over hours-to-days beats more chemistry. Multi-day for stubborn orange ghosts.
Cool machine wash. AIR-DRY FLAT — linen wrinkles aggressively when wet, hanging stretches the fiber. NEVER hot dryer until cleared.

steps: 1. Structured / vintage linen = pro from minute one. NO alkaline cleaners. NO hot water.
2. Scrape solids gently with plastic edge.
5. Diluted white vinegar 5–10 min — rinse thoroughly before peroxide.
6. 3% peroxide damp cloth on white linen only.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop on dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen (pro). Stop if hot water or hot dryer was used (often locks orange shadow). Stop if alkaline cleaner was used as first step (darkened tannin). Stop after multi-day sunlight plateau on dyed linen.
warnings: Structured / vintage linen = pro from minute one
NEVER hot water — sets tannin and lycopene
NEVER alkaline cleaners — DARKEN tannin permanently

neverDo: Never alkaline cleaners on tannin — darken permanently
Never hot water on tomato sauce + linen
Never skip Dawn oil-strip before peroxide
Never mix peroxide + chlorine + ammonia + alkaline
fiberSensitivities: White / light washable linen: tolerates Dawn + vinegar + 3% peroxide
Dyed linen: hidden test mandatory before peroxide
Structured / lined linen: pro — warps wet

materialWarnings: Order matters — Dawn oil-strip FIRST, then color treatment

No hot water — sets tannin and lycopene
Sunlight is the most reliable finisher for residual orange

escalation.when: Dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen. Hot water or hot dryer already used. Alkaline cleaner used as first step (darkened tannin). Multi-day sunlight plateau on dyed linen with orange shadow.
escalation.whatToTell: "Tomato sauce on [white / dyed / structured / vintage] linen, age [fresh / hours / hot-dried suspected], home-treated with [scrape + cold back-flush + Dawn oil-strip + diluted vinegar + 3% peroxide on white + sunlight × N hours/days + cool wash + air-dry flat]. No hot, no alkaline. [Residual orange / hot-dried suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with combination-stain (tannin + oil) chemistry

contextQuestions: Marinara, vodka sauce, pizza sauce, or with cream / cheese?
Simple flat linen, structured / lined, or vintage?
Has alkaline cleaner been used as first step?
Sunny location available for sunlight finisher?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Cream-based tomato sauces (vodka sauce) add the protein step (use coffee-cream-style ordering: protein FIRST). Pizza grease + tomato is heavier on the oil component — extended Dawn dwell. Sunlight is genuinely the most reliable finisher because lycopene is photodegradable. Structured / lined linen warps under wet handling — pro from minute one.
